title: Post-Graduate Diploma in Crime Scene Management
content: The Post-Graduate Diploma in Crime Scene Management is a one-year professional program designed to impart specialized skills in crime scene handling, evidence preservation, documentation, reconstruction, and chain of custody protocols. It combines rigorous theoretical instruction with hands-on training to prepare students for field and laboratory-based forensic roles. Graduation in relevant subjects with minimum 45% marks in aggregate Students having CUET Score will be given preference in merit. 01 Develop foundational and applied knowledge in crime scene investigation, including scene preservation, evidence recognition, and systematic documentation techniques. 02 Train students in the scientific methods of locating, collecting, packaging, and preserving physical evidence using standardized forensic protocols. 03 Equip learners with hands-on skills through simulated crime scenes and practical exercises in fingerprint lifting, bloodstain pattern analysis, and trace evidence recovery. 04 Foster interdisciplinary understanding of the roles of law enforcement, forensic experts, and legal professionals in crime scene management and chain of custody maintenance. 05 Prepare students for professional roles in police departments, forensic units, private investigative services, and crime scene response teams with strong ethical and legal awareness 01 Acquire advanced competencies in forensic scene processing and decision-making. 02 Be capable of leading or advising on complex crime scene investigations. 03 Ensure scientific integrity and legal admissibility of evidence. 04 Contribute to the justice system through precise, professional scene management practices.
